narrative: Dear [Student's Name], I know this rejection letter stings, especially after pouring so much into your law school dreams, and I want to walk you through why our prediction model saw your path to the bar exam as a tough one—77% chance of not passing, based on patterns from thousands of students like you. Picture it like a story the model pieced together from your profile: your Year 3 grades landing you in the bottom decile of your school stood out as the biggest red flag, whispering that the rigors of upper-level law courses might prove overwhelming. That early stumble in Year 1, with another bottom-decile finish, reinforced the narrative of building momentum being a challenge from the start. Your LSAT score of 20 added weight to that, signaling to the model a hurdle in the kind of quick analytical thinking the bar demands. Even your upper-middle family income, while a solid foundation, didn't quite offset the pattern in a way the model trusted, as it often sees privilege not fully bridging academic gaps like yours. Your Asian background played a role too, fitting into data trends where high expectations sometimes clash with these grade realities. Your undergraduate GPA of 2.7 echoed that theme of steady but not standout performance, and going full-time straight away painted a picture of potential burnout without proven resilience. Being a woman factored in subtly, aligning with patterns the model has seen in bar passage rates. It's this whole mosaic—your deciles pulling hardest toward struggle, layered with test scores and background—that convinced the model your odds weren't strong enough for us to bet on right now. But stories aren't set in stone; many have rewritten theirs by targeting those early grade patterns with focused prep or retakes. You've got target_law drive shining through—use it to build a stronger chapter ahead. We're rooting for your comeback.
